title: Output Head Architecture Audit
research_question: Now that the output path is proven important, what head architecture best turns hidden states into logits under the parameter-golf budget?

candidates.name: k1-low-rank-128
candidates.goal: Test whether a low-rank untied output head can keep most of the expressivity win while using fewer bytes.
candidates.hypothesis: The current untied dense head may be overbuilt, and a low-rank factorization with rank 128 can preserve the useful part of the gain.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether output expressivity can be made more parameter-efficient without collapsing quality.
candidates.why_worthy: The output path already gave some of the biggest wins in the project, so architectural compression here is high leverage.
candidates.description: replace the dense untied head with a rank-128 low-rank output head

candidates.name: k2-low-rank-64
candidates.goal: Push the low-rank idea harder to see where the head architecture breaks.
candidates.hypothesis: The logits may be structured enough that a rank-64 head still preserves most of the useful behavior.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether low-rank heads degrade gracefully or cliff sharply.
candidates.why_worthy: This gives a clean slope for the family instead of a single low-rank point.
candidates.description: replace the dense untied head with a rank-64 low-rank output head

candidates.name: k3-bottleneck-256
candidates.goal: Test a nonlinear bottleneck head instead of a plain linear map.
candidates.hypothesis: A two-stage bottleneck head may express a better logit mapping than a single dense matrix at similar cost.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the output path wants more structure, not just fewer parameters.
candidates.why_worthy: This is a genuinely different output-head mechanism, not just another rank value.
candidates.description: use a bottleneck output head with width 256 before logits

candidates.name: k4-tied-plus-residual
candidates.goal: Blend the efficiency of tied embeddings with a small learned corrective output path.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may not need a fully untied dense head if a tied base plus a small residual head captures the missing freedom.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the untied win is mostly about a small correction or about fully separate output weights.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a challenge-specific compromise between size and output expressivity.
candidates.description: use tied logits plus a small residual output head

candidates.name: k5-two-stage-256
candidates.goal: Test the boldest head in the tranche: a learned two-stage output mapper.
candidates.hypothesis: The output path may want its own hidden transformation before logits, especially in a tiny-model regime where the last projection is a bottleneck.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether richer output computation beats both dense and compressed linear heads.
candidates.why_worthy: If output path is really first-class, then a more expressive head could be a frontier move rather than a side tweak.
candidates.description: use a two-stage nonlinear output head with width 256
